questions.text: Which of these is a protozoan disease?
questions.explanation: Malaria is caused by Plasmodium, a protozoan. Typhoid and cholera are bacterial, influenza is viral.
Antibiotics work against bacteria, not viruses. Viral infections require antiviral drugs or vaccines.

questions.text: What is the principle of immunization?
questions.correctAnswer: Introducing weakened pathogens to develop immunity without causing disease.
questions.explanation: Immunization introduces weakened or dead pathogens to stimulate immune system to produce antibodies.
AIDS (Acquired Immuno Deficiency Syndrome) is caused by HIV (Human Immunodeficiency Virus).
